assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md states '## Classification: Code Change Request' and 'Action: Create sub-task (subtask-30001.md)'. report.md confirms in the Review Comments Summary table: '30001 | modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs | Code change request -- wrap updates in transaction | Yes (subtask-30001.md)'. The file subtask-30001.md exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md states '## Classification: Suggestion' and explicitly analyzes the suggestive language: '"should also" -- This is suggestive phrasing indicating an additional improvement', '"would help" -- This is explicitly suggestive, framing the index as something that would be helpful rather than something that is required', '"Something like" -- This frames the provided SQL as an example or rough idea, not a prescribed implementation'.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md states '## Classification: Nit' and 'Action: No sub-task created. Nits do not warrant follow-up tasks.' report.md confirms in Review Comments Summary: '30003 | modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs | Nit -- rename context message | No'. No subtask-30003.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md states '## Classification: Question' and 'Action: No sub-task created. Questions require author/team clarification, not autonomous code changes.' report.md confirms: '30004 | modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/get.rs | Question -- GET behavior for deleted SBOMs | No'. No subtask-30004.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 1, value 'trustify-backend'), '## Description' (line 5), '## Files to Modify' (line 9), '## Implementation Notes' (line 12), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 19). All sections have substantive content.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Target PR' section (line 25) with the exact URL 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744' on line 26.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Review Context' section (line 28) which includes 'Review comment #30001 by reviewer-a on modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs line 60:' followed by the full original review comment text: 'The soft_delete method should run all three UPDATE statements inside a single database transaction...'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only 6 files: report.md, review-30001.md through review-30004.md, and subtask-30001.md. All are markdown documentation/analysis files. No source code files (.rs, .toml, .sql, etc.) were created or appear in the outputs directory. The output files are classification reports, a sub-task description, and the verification report.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All four review comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) have individual classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md). The report.md Review Comments Summary table lists all four comments with classifications. The Findings section references comments 30001, 30002, and 30003 explicitly. Comment 30004 is referenced in the Observation section about get.rs not being modified.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains no merge commands, no auto-merge directives, and no merge-related actions. The verdict is 'FAIL -- Changes Requested by Reviewer', indicating the PR is not approved for merge. The report only contains analysis, classification, and sub-task creation -- no merge action.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains under '### Test Change Classification': '**Classification**: ADDITIVE' followed by 'All test changes are in a **new test file** (`tests/api/sbom_delete.rs`). No existing test files were modified.'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a full '### Convention Upgrade Evaluation' subsection that explicitly evaluates convention upgrade eligibility: 'The comment was evaluated for convention upgrade eligibility. For a suggestion to be upgraded to a code change request, there must be a project convention in the repository that backs the upgrade.' It then evaluates four sources of convention evidence and concludes: 'Without concrete project convention evidence to support an upgrade, the comment remains classified as a suggestion.' Additionally, report.md Findings section states: 'no project convention in the available fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request.'

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task — the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md concludes: 'Action: No sub-task created. Suggestions do not trigger sub-task creation unless upgraded by convention evidence, and no applicable convention was found.' report.md Review Comments Summary shows '30002 | migration/src/m0042_sbom_soft_delete/mod.rs | Suggestion -- add partial index | No'. No subtask-30002.md file exists in the outputs directory. The Findings section confirms: 'no project convention in the available fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request. The suggestion is noted but does not trigger a sub-task.'

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains under '### Eval Quality': '**Classification**: N/A' followed by 'No eval result reviews exist in this PR. No reviews match the 3-criteria detection pattern (author `github-actions[bot]`, marker `## Eval Results`, footer `sdlc-workflow/run-evals`).' All three detection criteria are explicitly listed. The Test Quality section proceeds independently without any Eval Quality impact.
